scenarios.sourceText: Maya shared that she finished her first 10K in heavy rain and thanked the volunteers who stayed to cheer.
scenarios.instruction: Congratulate her without sounding exaggerated.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent a finish time or race location.

scenarios.sourceText: A teammate says the revised onboarding draft is ready and asks whether you can review it by Tuesday afternoon.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm that Tuesday afternoon works.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not promise an earlier review.

scenarios.sourceText: A friend posted photos from a quiet weekend hike and said the fog made the trail feel completely different.
scenarios.instruction: Respond with genuine curiosity.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not name a trail or mountain.

scenarios.sourceText: The design note proposes keeping drafts only in memory so closing the workflow always clears the text.
scenarios.instruction: Agree while acknowledging the tradeoff.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim a database exists.

scenarios.sourceText: A customer says the export worked after they restarted the app and thanks the support team for the quick response.
scenarios.instruction: Reply appreciatively and close the loop.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not offer a refund or coupon.

scenarios.sourceText: The community library is collecting used science books until Friday for its new after-school shelf.
scenarios.instruction: Encourage participation in a grounded way.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent a drop-off address.

scenarios.sourceText: The project lead requests the final launch brief by Thursday at 3 PM and asks that the open analytics question be flagged.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm delivery and mention the open question.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not say the analytics question is resolved.

scenarios.sourceText: A vendor moved tomorrow's integration call from 10 AM to 11:30 AM and kept the same meeting link.
scenarios.instruction: Acknowledge the change succinctly.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not add attendees or an agenda.

scenarios.sourceText: The security review found no critical issues but asks for clearer documentation of token rotation before approval.
scenarios.instruction: Thank the reviewer and state the next action.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim approval has been granted.

scenarios.sourceText: A candidate confirms they can attend the technical interview on Monday at 2 PM Nepal time.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm the appointment warmly but formally.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not add interview length or panel names.

scenarios.sourceText: The weekly report shows mobile crash-free sessions improved from 98.7 percent to 99.2 percent after the patch.
scenarios.instruction: Recognize the improvement and suggest continued monitoring.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not attribute the result to a specific engineer.

scenarios.sourceText: The organizer asks speakers to submit final slide decks as PDF files no later than August 8.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm the requirement and deadline.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim the slides are already submitted.

scenarios.sourceText: Sam says their rescue dog finally learned to wait calmly at the front door after three weeks of practice.
scenarios.instruction: Celebrate the small win.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent the dog's name or breed.

scenarios.sourceText: A classmate shares that the study notes you sent helped them understand recursion before today's quiz.
scenarios.instruction: Reply modestly and wish them well.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim to know their quiz result.

scenarios.sourceText: A neighbor invites you to a rooftop potluck on Saturday and asks everyone to bring a vegetarian dish.
scenarios.instruction: Accept the invitation with enthusiasm.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not name the dish you will bring.

scenarios.sourceText: Leena posted that her tiny balcony tomatoes produced their first ripe fruit of the season.
scenarios.instruction: Share her excitement in one or two sentences.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent the tomato variety.

scenarios.sourceText: A teammate apologizes for missing the stand-up because their internet connection failed during a storm.
scenarios.instruction: Reassure them and ask for an update when convenient.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not imply they missed other work.

scenarios.sourceText: The local cafe announces that its reading corner is reopening tomorrow after new shelves were installed.
scenarios.instruction: Respond as a happy regular customer.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not mention menu items or opening hours.

scenarios.sourceText: A developer says the bug disappeared immediately after they began screen-sharing it with the team.
scenarios.instruction: Make a light debugging joke without being dismissive.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim the bug is permanently fixed.

scenarios.sourceText: A friend baked sourdough that expanded far beyond the pan but says it still tasted great.
scenarios.instruction: Tease the dramatic rise kindly.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent ingredients.

scenarios.sourceText: The office printer worked all week and then jammed five minutes before the client presentation.
scenarios.instruction: Use restrained workplace humor.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not blame a person or printer model.

scenarios.sourceText: Nico says their cat ignored a new bed and chose the cardboard delivery box instead.
scenarios.instruction: Reply with a playful observation.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent the cat's age or color.

scenarios.sourceText: A runner says their fitness watch celebrated a personal record while they were rushing through an airport.
scenarios.instruction: Make a quick joke and acknowledge the record.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not invent a distance or pace.

scenarios.sourceText: The team renamed a confusing variable and suddenly half the code review comments became unnecessary.
scenarios.instruction: Praise clarity with gentle technical humor.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not claim behavior or performance changed.

scenarios.sourceText: The venue confirms the room is reserved for Wednesday from 4 PM to 6 PM and seats 24 people.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm receipt in one sentence.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not add catering or equipment details.

scenarios.sourceText: A maintainer asks contributors to rerun the Android tests after updating the Kotlin dependency.
scenarios.instruction: Acknowledge the request in under 20 words.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not say the tests have already passed.

scenarios.sourceText: A friend asks whether dinner at 7:30 PM at the same restaurant still works for you.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm in a warm single sentence.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not change the plan.

scenarios.sourceText: The incident update says service is restored and monitoring will continue for the next two hours.
scenarios.instruction: Acknowledge recovery and monitoring in under 25 words.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not state a root cause.

scenarios.sourceText: A seller confirms the replacement cable shipped today and provides an estimated Friday delivery.
scenarios.instruction: Thank them briefly.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not mention tracking details.

scenarios.sourceText: The organizer says volunteer orientation starts at 9 AM sharp and asks everyone to arrive ten minutes early.
scenarios.instruction: Confirm the timing without filler.
scenarios.mustNotInvent: Do not add a location.
